title_working: The Wiretap That Cracked Wall Street
warnings: MID FEATURE (~30 min). target_duration_minutes=30 uses the 'mid' duration profile (ADR-0008): narration band 24.0-32.4 min; finished-runtime band 27-33 min (check_final_acceptance). NOT the standard 11.5-12.5 gate.

R2 — Raj Rajaratnam is living and was CONVICTED AT A JURY TRIAL (verdict May 11, 2011; 14 counts). Conviction and sentence are stateable as fact; conduct framed as 'the jury found / prosecutors proved / the government said'. PRE-PUBLISH LEGAL REVIEW REQUIRED before any public release.

DO NOT conflate co-defendants. Rajat Gupta is a SEPARATE case (convicted June 2012; 2 years + $5M fine) and must never be merged with Rajaratnam's conduct or penalties. Tippers (Goel/Intel, Kumar/McKinsey, Moffat/IBM, Chiesi/New Castle) charged separately. SEC $92.8M civil penalty kept distinct from the $53,816,434 forfeiture + $10M fine.

Profit is scope-dependent; on screen use the court forfeiture '$53.8 million' / 'more than $50 million', never a single invented flat number. The 'longest insider-trading sentence' superlative is attributed to prosecutors and time-bound to 2011.

Left process (Claude) complete through script_verified: topic + sources(15) + claims(20) + script + annotated(53 spans) + script_qc + codex image prompts + design docs. NEXT human gate = APR-0001 owner script approval (currently PENDING). No paid API, render, upload, or publish performed.
No real-person likeness or real logos/seals/landmarks in any generated image (invariant 11). Codex image generation only; SDXL not used for this episode's hero stills.
